metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a step (and a corresponding AC or sub-AC) that describes a test exercising the persistence-failure branch: mock the sidecar write to raise an exception and assert the close path still returns/raises the FAIL verdict unchanged.
findings.finding: AC3 ('Persistence failure does not change the close outcome') has no described implementation step or test that exercises the failure branch — the plan states the invariant but neither the What section nor the ACs map a concrete step or test to it.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3; What section (persist-and-block prose)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'A second test mocks the sidecar writer to raise an exception and asserts the close path still blocks (raises/returns the FAIL verdict) and does not swallow the error.' Update AC3 to reference this test explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `schema` field (e.g. `"completion_verifier_fail_v1"`) to the required sidecar payload fields list, so readers can identify and version-guard the new record type.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify a payload schema version string for the new sidecar record. The existing REVIEW_RESULT sidecars use 'plan_review_result_v1' (plan_review/sidecar.py) and 'code_review_result_v1' (code_review/sidecar.py); the readers use the schema field as a guard to skip malformed or foreign-schema payloads (see sidecar.py:157 'payload.get("schema") == "plan_review_result_v1"'). Without a named schema, a future reader has no stable schema guard to rely on, and the offline calibration tooling cannot safely distinguish completion-verifier FAIL records from other REVIEW_RESULT payloads on the same ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Include' list, add: 'schema: "completion_verifier_fail_v1" (for reader schema-guard compatibility, following the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar precedent)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC requiring a test that injects a persistence failure (e.g., storage write raises) and asserts the close outcome is still FAIL (fail-closed preserved), covering AC3.
findings.finding: AC3 (persistence failure must not mask the FAIL) is a behavioral requirement for the new logic, but no corresponding test is specified. The test AC covers only the success path of persistence ('assert the record exists with the expected fields'); the failure sub-path — where the storage write itself throws — has no AC-level test requirement.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 ('Persistence failure does not change the close outcome (fail-closed preserved)') and AC2 ('A test drives a mocked FAIL … and asserts the record exists')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: '- [ ] A test injects a storage-write failure during the FAIL-persistence step and asserts the close attempt still returns/raises the original FAIL verdict (fail-closed preserved).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the new completion-verifier FAIL sidecar event type to both `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py` `EVENT_TYPES` and `src/rebar/reducer/_version.py` `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` (required for emit to succeed and for fsck not to warn).
findings.finding: The plan does not enumerate two mandatory infrastructure edit targets: (1) `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py` `EVENT_TYPES` allow-list, and (2) `src/rebar/reducer/_version.py` `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES`. Any new sidecar event type (e.g. COMPLETION_FAIL) must be added to EVENT_TYPES or `append_event` raises `StoreError('invalid event_type')` at emit time, silently failing. It must also be added to `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` or `fsck`/`bridge_fsck` will permanently warn 'newer than me' on every integrity check.
findings.location: What section — 'mirror the plan-review REVIEW_RESULT sidecar pattern'
findings.suggested_fix: In `_store/event_append.py`, add the new type (e.g. `"COMPLETION_FAIL"`) to the `EVENT_TYPES` frozenset with a comment matching the REVIEW_RESULT entry. In `reducer/_version.py`, add the same string to `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES`. Both changes mirror the REVIEW_RESULT precedent exactly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the new event type token (e.g. `COMPLETION_FAIL`) and explicitly call out that it must be registered in both `_store/event_append.py::EVENT_TYPES` and `reducer/_version.py::_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` (mirroring the dual-registration the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar uses).
findings.finding: The plan instructs the implementer to 'mirror the plan-review REVIEW_RESULT sidecar pattern' but never names the new event type token (e.g. `COMPLETION_FAIL` or `VERIFY_RESULT`). The existing infrastructure requires the token to be registered in at least two places simultaneously — `_store/event_append.py::EVENT_TYPES` (the write-path allowlist; missing registration raises `StoreError` at runtime) and `reducer/_version.py::_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` (fsck forward-compat recognition; missing registration generates a fsck warning on every node that hasn't upgraded). Without calling this out, an implementer may register only one location and ship a silently broken path.
findings.location: ## What — 'mirror the plan-review REVIEW_RESULT sidecar pattern (an event/sidecar in the ticket store keyed to the ticket)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a bullet to the ## What section: 'Register the new event type token (e.g. `COMPLETION_FAIL`) in `_store/event_append.py::EVENT_TYPES` (write-path allowlist) AND `reducer/_version.py::_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` (fsck forward-compat recognition), exactly as REVIEW_RESULT is registered in both.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the new sidecar event type explicitly (e.g. `COMPLETION_VERIFIER_RESULT`) and specify that it must be added to both `_store/event_append.py:EVENT_TYPES` and `reducer/_version.py:_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES`.
findings.finding: The plan never names the new store event type for the FAIL-verdict sidecar. The ticket-store write path (`_store/event_append.py:EVENT_TYPES` frozenset + `_validate_event`) rejects any event type not in its explicit allow-list with a hard `StoreError`, and `reducer/_version.py:_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` must also include the new type so `fsck` does not warn about it as 'newer than me'. Every existing sidecar type ('REVIEW_RESULT', 'TICKET_DIGEST', 'ENQUEUE_ENRICH', etc.) is registered in both files by its exact string name. The plan says 'mirror the plan-review REVIEW_RESULT sidecar pattern' but never specifies whether to reuse 'REVIEW_RESULT', introduce 'COMPLETION_VERIFIER_RESULT', or use another name. An executor cannot write the persistence code or the registration commits without this name.
findings.location: What section: 'mirror the plan-review REVIEW_RESULT sidecar pattern (an event/sidecar in the ticket store keyed to the ticket)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'What' section: 'Use a new reducer-ignored event type `COMPLETION_VERIFIER_RESULT` (mirroring `REVIEW_RESULT`): add it to `_store/event_append.EVENT_TYPES` and `reducer._version._NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` so the write path accepts it and fsck does not warn about it.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a concrete proving command for AC1, e.g. a CLI invocation or `find`/`jq` command that locates and validates the persisted sidecar record for a given ticket ID after a FAIL.
findings.finding: AC1 ('a durable, queryable record') has no concrete proving command — 'queryable' is undefined (no CLI command, jq path, or file-glob specified) and the plan provides no way to verify AC1 is satisfied beyond passing a mocked test.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC1: 'Verified by: `rebar ticket show <ticket-id> --sidecars | jq -e \'.["completion_fail"] | .findings | length > 0\'` (or equivalent) returning exit 0 after a failed close attempt.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or integration test step) that performs an actual sidecar write to a real (or test-fixture) ticket store instance and reads back the record, proving the live persistence path works end-to-end.
findings.finding: AC2 exercises the new persistence path only via a mock ('a mocked FAIL through the close path'), meaning the real ticket-store write is never exercised by any acceptance criterion — the live persistence boundary is bypassed.

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that the new completion-verifier FAIL sidecar reuses the existing `REVIEW_RESULT` event type (as `code_review/sidecar.py` already does) with a distinct payload `schema` field (e.g. `"completion_verifier_fail_v1"`), rather than introducing a new event type that would require updating `EVENT_TYPES`, `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES`, and fsck.
findings.finding: The plan says 'mirror the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar pattern' but leaves implicit whether the implementation should reuse the existing REVIEW_RESULT event type (already in EVENT_TYPES, _NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES, and the write-path allow-list — zero infrastructure changes, as code_review/sidecar.py already does) or introduce a new event type (e.g. COMPLETION_FAIL), which requires updating at least EVENT_TYPES in _store/event_append.py, _NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES in reducer/_version.py, and fsck forward-compat handling. The first option is clearly simpler and more codebase-aligned; the ambiguity could lead the implementer toward unnecessary infrastructure churn.
findings.location: What section: 'mirror the plan-review REVIEW_RESULT sidecar pattern'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'Reuse the existing REVIEW_RESULT event type (as code_review/sidecar.py already does) with a distinct payload schema field (e.g. "completion_verifier_fail_v1") — no changes to EVENT_TYPES, _NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES, or fsck are needed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `timestamp` field name, type, and source for the persisted FAIL record (e.g. ISO-8601 UTC string from `datetime.now(UTC).isoformat()`, or explicitly rely on the HLC event filename prefix and drop it from the payload field list).
findings.finding: The plan's 'What' section lists 'timestamp' as a required field in the persisted FAIL record, but the `completion_verdict` schema (`src/rebar/schemas/completion_verdict.schema.json`) — the implied shape for the persisted payload — has no `timestamp` property and defines no semantics for it. The schema is `additionalProperties: true` so extra fields are accepted, but no field name, type, or semantics (wall-clock ISO 8601? Unix epoch nanoseconds? HLC integer?) is specified. The calibration audit use-case explicitly depends on timestamps for temporal ordering and correlation.
findings.location: What section: 'Include: verdict, findings[] (criterion, finding, remediation), certifiable, model/runner, timestamp, and the ticket's material fingerprint at verify time.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC1 (or AC2's assertion list) to explicitly require the material fingerprint field in the persisted record, matching the field enumerated in the What section.
findings.finding: The 'What' section specifies that the persisted record must include 'the ticket's material fingerprint at verify time', but no acceptance criterion verifies this field is present. AC1 only checks for 'failing criteria and per-finding remediation text', leaving the fingerprint field uncovered.
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC1 to: 'A FAILed close attempt leaves a durable, queryable record containing: verdict, failing criteria, per-finding remediation text, certifiable flag, model/runner, timestamp, and the ticket's material fingerprint at verify time.' Then add a corresponding assertion in AC2's test.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the sidecar key/filename convention and serialization format explicitly in the What section (e.g. 'JSON, following the same naming scheme as REVIEW_RESULT sidecars: <ticket-id>/sidecars/completion_fail_<iso-timestamp>.json').
findings.finding: The sidecar's storage key format, file-naming convention, and serialization format are not stated — 'mirror the plan-review REVIEW_RESULT sidecar pattern' is a reference an executor must resolve by reading the codebase, not a stated default in the plan.
findings.location: What section — 'mirror the plan-review REVIEW_RESULT sidecar pattern'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the FAIL-verdict sidecar write is idempotent (e.g., keyed by ticket-id + attempt-id/content-hash so retries are safe no-ops or explicit upserts).
findings.finding: The plan does not specify that writes of the FAIL-verdict sidecar are idempotent. If a user retries a failed close attempt (e.g., network blip after the persistence write but before the blocking response), a second sidecar record for the same ticket+attempt could be written, corrupting the calibration dataset with duplicate entries.
findings.suggested_fix: Key the sidecar file/record on `<ticket_id>_<sha256_of_verdict_content[:8]>` or include a stable attempt-id so a re-run produces an identical key and the write is a safe overwrite rather than an append.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit requirement that a persistence failure is surfaced (at minimum a logged WARNING with the exception, ideally also reflected in the CLI output) so operators can detect and remediate missing records.
findings.finding: The plan requires that persistence failure must not mask the FAIL verdict (AC #3), but does not specify that the persistence failure itself is surfaced — logged, emitted as a warning, or otherwise observable. An operator seeing a FAIL with no corresponding record would have no way to distinguish 'record was never written' from 'record was lost' without additional diagnostic context.
findings.location: AC #3 / What section ('persistence failure must not mask the FAIL')
findings.suggested_fix: In the close path's persistence block, catch the write exception, log it at WARNING level with ticket ID and exception detail, then re-raise (or surface inline) before returning the FAIL result to the caller.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Require the new sidecar schema to be captured in a versioned artifact (e.g., a TypedDict/dataclass in the codebase, a JSON Schema file, or a CLAUDE.md section) so the field contract is stable and discoverable.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new queryable sidecar type with a specific schema (verdict, findings[], criterion, finding, remediation, certifiable, model/runner, timestamp, material fingerprint) but does not require this schema to be captured in any design doc, CLAUDE.md, or schema file. Future consumers querying these records offline have no authoritative reference for the field contract.
findings.suggested_fix: Define a `VerifierFailRecord` dataclass or TypedDict in a shared schema module (e.g., `src/rebar/schema/verifier_fail.py`) and serialise/deserialise via it; reference the module path in CLAUDE.md under 'ticket store sidecar types'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the FAIL verdict sidecar write is idempotent (e.g., keyed by ticket-id + attempt timestamp/run-id with an upsert/CAS, or a uniqueness constraint enforced by the store) so that retried or concurrent close attempts do not produce duplicate records.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify whether the FAIL verdict record write is idempotent under retry or safe under concurrent close attempts. If a close attempt writes the record and then fails (e.g., the persistence succeeds but the command exits unclean), a retry will write a second record for the same ticket. Similarly, two concurrent close attempts on the same ticket could race to write duplicate records. The plan says 'persist-and-block' but gives no uniqueness/deduplication guarantee.
findings.location: ## What — 'persist the full verdict durably' / ## Acceptance Criteria — 'Persistence failure does not change the close outcome'
findings.suggested_fix: Key the sidecar record by (ticket_id, close_attempt_id) where close_attempt_id is generated once at the start of the close path and reused on retry; use an upsert/CAS write so a re-run of the same attempt overwrites rather than appends.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
